pattern: Repeatedly trading in empty bottles for full ones is a self-similar process whose total yield has a closed form: each unit of exchange rate beyond the first effectively 'costs' one bottle to manufacture one extra drink.
hints: Every bottle you drink becomes an empty bottle you might later exchange - simulate rounds of drinking then exchanging until you can't exchange anymore.
Track how many full bottles you currently have and how many empty ones you're holding; each round, convert as many empty bottles as possible into new full ones.
There is also a direct formula: total = numBottles + floor((numBottles - 1) / (numExchange - 1)), since each new bottle earned only 'costs' numExchange-1 net empties to produce.

edge_inputs.description: one round of exchange leaves one bottle over
only one bottle, never enough to exchange
exchange rate equal to the starting count, exactly one extra bottle
the cheapest possible exchange rate, maximizing extra bottles
exchange rate one more than the bottle count, never enough to exchange even once
a single bottle against the most expensive exchange rate

stress.description: random valid bottle counts and exchange rates across the full range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    numBottles = rng.randint(1, 100)
    numExchange = rng.randint(2, 100)
    return [numBottles, numExchange]
